austinSymphony.title: Brahms and the Nordic Masterpiece
austinSymphony.program: Begin the season with music that balances clarity and intensity. Featuring Theofanidis’ Rainbow Body, Brahms’ Violin Concerto with Geneva Lewis, and Sibelius’ Fifth Symphony, this program offers depth, structure, and expressive range.

austinSymphony.title: Halloween Children's Concert
austinSymphony.program: Celebrate Halloween with Prokofiev’s Peter and the Wolf, the thrilling tale of a brave boy, his animal friends, and a fearsome wolf, all brought to life by the instruments of the orchestra.

austinSymphony.title: To Have and to Hold
austinSymphony.program: An exploration of love and marriage through music and narration. Led by Jamie Bernstein, daughter of the great Leonard Bernstein, this program blends Trouble in Tahiti with works by Strauss, Mendelssohn, and others, offering a thoughtful and engaging concert experience.

austinSymphony.title: An Evening with Yo-Yo Ma
austinSymphony.program: For one night only, Yo-Yo Ma performs Dvořák’s Cello Concerto in a rare Austin appearance. With music by Hovhaness and Bizet, this program offers a limited opportunity to experience one of today’s most respected artists live.

austinSymphony.title: Elf in Concert
austinSymphony.program: Nobody spreads Christmas cheer quite like Buddy the Elf! This holiday season, your Austin Symphony Orchestra brings Elf in Concert to Dell Hall at the Long Center, performing John Debney’s heartwarming score live as the full film plays on the big screen.

austinSymphony.title: Pictures at an Exhibition
austinSymphony.program: A program centered on transformation and imagination. Featuring Brahms’ Handel Variations, Liszt’s Piano Concerto with Yifei Xu, and Mussorgsky’s Pictures at an Exhibition, this concert explores evolving musical ideas through structure, color, and character.

austinSymphony.title: Joshua Bell Plays Beethoven
austinSymphony.program: Joshua Bell performs Beethoven’s Violin Concerto in a program that also includes Verdi’s La forza del destino overture and Lutosławski’s Concerto for Orchestra. A concert that explores contrast, structure, and orchestral color.

austinSymphony.title: Daphnis & Gloria
austinSymphony.program: A program for chorus and orchestra featuring Mazzoli's The Shield of the Heart is the Heart, Poulenc’s Gloria with Chorus Austin, and Ravel’s Daphnis et Chloé. This concert explores voice, texture, and the connection between contemporary and classical traditions.

austinSymphony.title: Mahler's 9th
austinSymphony.program: Mahler’s Symphony No. 9 is presented in a program led by Peter Bay. This performance highlights the work’s structure, emotional range, and sense of reflection, offering an immersive orchestral experience.

austinSymphony.title: Ode to Joy
austinSymphony.program: A program for chorus, soloists, and orchestra featuring Beethoven’s Symphony No. 9 and a narrated work based on Mozart. This concert focuses on connection, shared experience, and the role of music in community.

austinSymphony.title: Star Wars: The Force Awakens in Concert
With Love and Gratitude
austinSymphony.program: The season concludes with works by Puts, Mozart, and Rachmaninoff. Featuring two-piano soloists Anton Nel and Jon Kimura Parker, this program reflects collaboration, balance, and a thoughtful close to Peter Bay’s tenure.

earlyMusicAustin.title: Eurotour: A Medieval Odyssey, c. 1100-1450
earlyMusicAustin.program: From the chants of Hildegard von Bingen to the polyphonic brilliance of Guillaume de Machaut, and from the raucous Italian dances of The Decameron to the subtle creations of the courts of Burgundy - the best and most characteristic Medieval music from England, France, Germany, Spain, and Italy.
earlyMusicAustin.venue_name: Redeemer Presbyterian Church (Sat) / St. Martin's Lutheran Church (Sun)

earlyMusicAustin.title: Joy and Light: Delights of the Season
earlyMusicAustin.program: TEMP's annual feast of holiday music for Christmas and Chanukah through the ages: medieval chant and joyous carols, magnificent motets, sweet Celtic songs, and exuberant folk-tunes for Chanukah.
earlyMusicAustin.venue_name: Redeemer Presbyterian Church (Sat) / St. Martin's Lutheran Church (Sun)

earlyMusicAustin.title: Eurotour: Renaissance Masters, c. 1450-1600
earlyMusicAustin.program: Sophisticated selections from the early Renaissance, featuring the Franco-Flemish masters through the magnificence of Italian multi-choral works and experiments that stretched the tonal landscape.

earlyMusicAustin.title: Eurotour: Early Baroque Pearls
earlyMusicAustin.program: The drama inherent in the expression of text and human emotions in the early Baroque music of Monteverdi, Strozzi, Charpentier, Schutz, and others - theatrical presentations, instrumental virtuosity, and vocal contrasts.
earlyMusicAustin.venue_name: Redeemer Presbyterian Church (Sat) / St. Martin's Lutheran Church (Sun)

austinChamberMusic.title: Invoke Quartet
austinChamberMusic.program: Austin Chamber Music Festival 2026 concert with the Invoke Quartet at Bates Recital Hall.

austinChamberMusic.title: Carnival of the Animals
austinChamberMusic.program: A family-friendly Austin Chamber Music Festival performance of Camille Saint-Saens's beloved Carnival of the Animals at Bates Recital Hall, with two showings at noon and 2 PM.

austinChamberMusic.title: Adult Division - Summer Workshop Concert
austinChamberMusic.program: Culmination concert of the Austin Chamber Music Center Summer Workshop's Adult Division at Bates Recital Hall.

austinChamberMusic.title: Primary Division - Summer Workshop Concert
austinChamberMusic.program: Culmination concert of the Austin Chamber Music Center Summer Workshop's Primary Division at Bates Recital Hall.

austinChamberMusic.title: Young Artists Division Chamber Ensembles Concert
austinChamberMusic.program: Culmination chamber ensembles concert of the Austin Chamber Music Center Summer Workshop's Young Artists Division at Bates Recital Hall.

austinChamberMusic.title: Young Artists Division Large Ensembles Concert
austinChamberMusic.program: Culmination large ensembles concert of the Austin Chamber Music Center Summer Workshop's Young Artists Division at Bates Recital Hall.

austinChamberMusic.title: Galvin Cello Quartet
austinChamberMusic.program: Austin Chamber Music Festival 2026 concert with the Galvin Cello Quartet at Bates Recital Hall.

austinChamberMusic.title: Ariel Quartet
austinChamberMusic.program: Austin Chamber Music Festival 2026 closing concert with the Ariel Quartet at Bates Recital Hall.

austinOpera.title: Ofrenda
austinOpera.program: World premiere commission opening Austin Opera's new Sarah and Ernest Butler Performance Center. A locally nurtured new opera honoring heritage and community.
austinOpera.venue_name: Sarah and Ernest Butler Performance Center

austinOpera.title: Thaïs
austinOpera.program: Massenet's lush tale of a monk's obsession with the courtesan Thaïs arrives in an exclusive Texas premiere production.
austinOpera.venue_name: Long Center for the Performing Arts

austinOpera.title: Fellow Travelers
austinOpera.program: Gregory Spears's acclaimed opera of love and persecution in McCarthy-era Washington, D.C.
austinOpera.venue_name: Long Center for the Performing Arts

austinOpera.title: La traviata
austinOpera.program: Verdi's beloved story of Violetta's sacrifice and doomed love, closing Austin Opera's 2026-27 season.
austinOpera.venue_name: Long Center for the Performing Arts

artsOnAlexander.title: Holbrook Organ Series | Christopher Brauckmann
artsOnAlexander.program: Organ recital in the Holbrook Organ Series.

artsOnAlexander.title: Bach & Beer
artsOnAlexander.program: Partita in A minor for Flute, BWV 1013; Sonatas for Flute and Obbligato Harpsichord, BWV 1030, 1034, 1035; solo harpsichord selections performed by Guy Whatley.

artsOnAlexander.title: Pulcinella
artsOnAlexander.program: Philharmonie Austin under Mark Dupere performs Prokofiev's Symphony No. 1 'Classical', Ravel's Le Tombeau de Couperin, and Stravinsky's Pulcinella Suite.
works: Symphony No. 1 in D Major, Op. 25 'Classical'

artsOnAlexander.title: Holbrook Organ Series | Katherine Meloan
artsOnAlexander.program: Organ recital in the Holbrook Organ Series.

artsOnAlexander.title: Vox Luminis | Vespers of 1910
artsOnAlexander.program: Vox Luminis performs Monteverdi's Vespro della Beata Vergine, SV 206.

artsOnAlexander.title: Holbrook Organ Series | Chris Oelkers
artsOnAlexander.program: Organ recital in the Holbrook Organ Series.

artsOnAlexander.title: A Festival of Nine Lessons and Carols
artsOnAlexander.program: Philharmonie Austin Chorus performs A Festival of Nine Lessons and Carols under the direction of Mark Dupere.

artsOnAlexander.title: Anton Nel - Chopin & Friends
artsOnAlexander.program: Solo works for piano performed on an 1855 Erard grand piano.

artsOnAlexander.title: Dixit Dominus
artsOnAlexander.program: Philharmonie Austin and Philharmonie Austin Chorus under Mark Dupere perform Handel's Dixit Dominus, HWV 232, a Vivaldi L'estro armonico concerto, and Bach's Christ lag in Todesbanden, BWV 4.
artsOnAlexander.featured_artist: Philharmonie Austin, Philharmonie Austin Chorus, Mark Dupere (conductor)

artsOnAlexander.title: VOCES8 | Towards Paradise
artsOnAlexander.program: Towards Paradise traces a journey through music of longing, faith, consolation, and peace; a pilgrimage towards rest, light, and eternity.

artsOnAlexander.title: Holbrook Organ Series | Midori Ataka
artsOnAlexander.program: Organ recital in the Holbrook Organ Series.

artsOnAlexander.title: Matthew Passion
artsOnAlexander.program: Philharmonie Austin and Philharmonie Austin Chorus perform Johann Sebastian Bach's St. Matthew Passion, BWV 244, under the direction of Mark Dupere.
artsOnAlexander.featured_artist: Philharmonie Austin, Philharmonie Austin Chorus, Mark Dupere (conductor)

artsOnAlexander.title: All Mozart!
artsOnAlexander.program: Oboe Quartet in F Major, Clarinet Quintet in A Major, and Divertimento for String Quartet.
